title: lophophora lewinii
intro: Lophophora lewinii, also known as the peyote cactus or small peyote, is a small, spineless cactus species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It is a psychoactive plant, containing the alkaloids mescaline, peyocactine, and anhalonidine, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine for their medicinal and spiritual properties. The health benefits of Lophophora lewinii include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and increase feelings of calmness and well-being. In herbal preparations, Lophophora lewinii is often dried and ground into a powder, or used to make teas and infusions, although its use is heavily regulated and should only be undertaken under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
